hero_tagline: Finding the perfect holiday park for your family's Dunsborough adventure means discovering a place where the kids can play freely while you relax and enjoy the stunning natural beauty together.
heading: Best Family Holiday & Caravan Parks in Dunsborough
hero_intro:
Planning a family trip to Dunsborough? We compared the best family holiday and caravan parks in Dunsborough so you can quickly find the right park for your kids, budget and travel style.
Dunsborough sits at the northern tip of the Margaret River wine region with some of WA's clearest, calmest water, and BIG4 Dunsborough Southern Stars runs one of the best-equipped parks in the entire south west.
local_knowledge: BIG4 Dunsborough Southern Stars scores 88/100, with an adventure playground, flying fox and BMX track on spacious grass sites, though it sits 10 to 15 minutes from the beach rather than right on it. Tasman Holiday Parks Yallingup Beach trades those facilities for absolute beachfront position, right on the sand with striking coastal views, better suited to families with smaller rigs prioritising location. Meelup and Eagle Bay, just north of town, offer some of the calmest, clearest swimming water on this coast, genuinely excellent for young children. Whale watching runs from Dunsborough and nearby Busselton during the migration season, and the Cape Naturaliste lighthouse adds an easy, scenic walk.

if_we_were_booking.label: Facilities and space
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose BIG4 Dunsborough Southern Stars for the adventure playground, flying fox and BMX track on spacious sites, accepting a short drive to the beach.

if_we_were_booking.label: Beachfront position
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose Tasman Holiday Parks Yallingup Beach for direct sand access and dramatic coastal views, trading facilities for location.

if_we_were_booking.label: Our tip
if_we_were_booking.text: Swim at Meelup or Eagle Bay rather than the more exposed surf beaches, the water here is exceptionally calm and clear, genuinely some of the best family swimming conditions in WA.

faq.question: Is Dunsborough good for families?
faq.answer: Yes, BIG4 Dunsborough Southern Stars is one of the best-equipped parks in the south west, and the calm bays at Meelup and Eagle Bay offer excellent swimming.

faq.question: Which Dunsborough park is best for kids?
faq.answer: BIG4 Dunsborough Southern Stars, scoring 88/100, with an adventure playground, flying fox and BMX track.

faq.question: Where is the best swimming for young children?
faq.answer: Meelup Beach and Eagle Bay, just north of Dunsborough, offer exceptionally calm, clear water.

faq.question: Is BIG4 Dunsborough Southern Stars close to the beach?
faq.answer: It's 10 to 15 minutes drive from the beach, trading beachfront position for spacious sites and comprehensive facilities.

faq.question: Which park is right on the beach?
faq.answer: Tasman Holiday Parks Yallingup Beach sits directly on the sand, suited to families with smaller rigs prioritising location.

faq.question: Can you see whales from Dunsborough?
faq.answer: Yes, whale watching cruises operate from Dunsborough and nearby Busselton during the migration season.

faq.question: What are the best free family activities?
faq.answer: Swimming at Meelup and Eagle Bay, and the walk to the Cape Naturaliste lighthouse.

faq.question: When is the best time to visit?
faq.answer: Summer for swimming, or the whale season months for an additional wildlife activity.

faq.question: What should families do when it rains?
faq.answer: BIG4 Dunsborough Southern Stars' games facilities cover a wet day, and the Margaret River region's indoor attractions are a short drive away.

faq.question: How far ahead should you book?
faq.answer: BIG4 Dunsborough Southern Stars books out well ahead for school holidays given its reputation.
